records.ety: [OE. contriven, contreven, controven, to invent, OF. controver, contruver; con- + trouver to find. See Troubadour, trover.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Contrived (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Contriving.]

defs: To form by an exercise of ingenuity; to devise; to invent; to design; to plan.
quotes: What more likely to contrive this admirable frame of the universe than infinite wisdom.
neither do thou imagine that I shall contrive aught against his life.

defs: To make devices; to form designs; to plan; to scheme; to plot.
quotes: The Fates with traitors do contrive.
Thou hast contrived against th very life Of the defendant.
